description: Having an even surface without bumps or ridges; flowing without interruption. 'Helu' names the quality of frictionless passage—surfaces that glide, transitions that flow, movement without obstacle.
sound_symbolism: The word glides without friction. 'He' begins with open breath, air passing freely. Then 'lu' flows liquid and easy, the smooth continuation.
grammatical_notes: An adjective: 'helu kerou' (smooth stone). Smoothness, the noun, comes by the quality-noun rule; smoothing is caused becoming ('ka kelu'). Related to 'luwae' (graceful) in implying ease of flow.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Smooth mind, smooth action—the quality of moving through life without creating friction or resistance.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Smooth curves and flowing lines defined Art Nouveau—surfaces without harsh edges, forms that flow into each other.
